thread_pool.c(3) Library Functions Manual thread_pool.c(3)

NAME

thread_pool.c - 🧵 Generic thread pool implementation for managing worker threads

SYNOPSIS

Functions


thread_pool_t * thread_pool_create (const char *pool_name)
thread_pool_t * thread_pool_create_with_workers (const char *pool_name, size_t num_workers)
void thread_pool_destroy (thread_pool_t *pool)
asciichat_error_t thread_pool_queue_work (const char *name, thread_pool_t *pool, void *(*work_func)(void *), void *work_arg)
asciichat_error_t thread_pool_spawn (thread_pool_t *pool, void *(*thread_func)(void *), void *thread_arg, int stop_id, const char *thread_name)
asciichat_error_t thread_pool_stop_all (thread_pool_t *pool)
size_t thread_pool_get_count (const thread_pool_t *pool)
bool thread_pool_has_threads (const thread_pool_t *pool)
asciichat_error_t thread_pool_interrupt_all (thread_pool_t *pool, int sig)

Detailed Description

🧵 Generic thread pool implementation for managing worker threads

Definition in file thread_pool.c.

Function Documentation

thread_pool_t * thread_pool_create (const char * pool_name)

Definition at line 74 of file thread_pool.c.

74                                                          {
75   thread_pool_t *pool = SAFE_MALLOC(sizeof(thread_pool_t), thread_pool_t *);
76   if (!pool) {
77     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate thread pool");
78     return NULL;
79   }
80 
81   memset(pool, 0, sizeof(*pool));
82 
83   // Copy pool name (truncate if necessary)
84   if (pool_name) {
85     SAFE_STRNCPY(pool->name, pool_name, sizeof(pool->name));
86   } else {
87     SAFE_STRNCPY(pool->name, "unnamed", sizeof(pool->name));
88   }
89 
90   // Initialize linked list
91   pool->threads = NULL;
92   pool->thread_count = 0;
93   pool->is_work_queue_mode = false;
94   pool->num_workers = 0;
95 
96   // Initialize mutex
97   if (mutex_init(&pool->threads_mutex, "thread_pool") != 0) {
98     SAFE_FREE(pool);
99     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_THREAD, "Failed to initialize thread pool mutex");
100     return NULL;
101   }
102 
103   NAMED_REGISTER_THREAD_POOL(pool, pool->name, NULL);
104 
105   // Register sync primitives for debugging
106   NAMED_REGISTER_MUTEX(&pool->threads_mutex, "threads_mutex", (uintptr_t)(const void *)(pool));
107 
108   log_debug("Thread pool '%s' created (long-lived thread mode)", pool->name);
109   return pool;
110 }

    tcp_server_add_client().






thread_pool_t
  * thread_pool_create_with_workers (const char * pool_name, size_t
  num_workers)


Definition at line 112 of file thread_pool.c.


112                                                                                           {
113   if (num_workers == 0) {
114     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "num_workers must be > 0");
115     return NULL;
116   }
117 
118   thread_pool_t *pool = SAFE_MALLOC(sizeof(thread_pool_t), thread_pool_t *);
119   if (!pool) {
120     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate thread pool");
121     return NULL;
122   }
123 
124   memset(pool, 0, sizeof(*pool));
125 
126   // Copy pool name
127   if (pool_name) {
128     SAFE_STRNCPY(pool->name, pool_name, sizeof(pool->name));
129   } else {
130     SAFE_STRNCPY(pool->name, "unnamed", sizeof(pool->name));
131   }
132 
133   // Mark as work queue mode
134   pool->is_work_queue_mode = true;
135   pool->num_workers = num_workers;
136   pool->work_queue = NULL;
137   pool->shutdown_requested = false;
138 
139   // Initialize mutexes
140   if (mutex_init(&pool->threads_mutex, "worker_list") != 0) {
141     SAFE_FREE(pool);
142     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_THREAD, "Failed to initialize threads_mutex");
143     return NULL;
144   }
145 
146   if (mutex_init(&pool->work_queue_mutex, "pending_tasks") != 0) {
147     mutex_destroy(&pool->threads_mutex);
148     SAFE_FREE(pool);
149     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_THREAD, "Failed to initialize work_queue_mutex");
150     return NULL;
151   }
152 
153   // Initialize condition variable
154   if (cond_init(&pool->work_available, "task_available") != 0) {
155     mutex_destroy(&pool->work_queue_mutex);
156     mutex_destroy(&pool->threads_mutex);
157     SAFE_FREE(pool);
158     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_THREAD, "Failed to initialize work_available condition");
159     return NULL;
160   }
161 
162   // Create worker threads
163   for (size_t i = 0; i < num_workers; i++) {
164     thread_pool_entry_t *entry = SAFE_MALLOC(sizeof(thread_pool_entry_t), thread_pool_entry_t *);
165     if (!entry) {
166       log_error("Failed to allocate worker entry %zu", i);
167       thread_pool_destroy(pool);
168       SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate worker entry");
169       return NULL;
170     }
171 
172     memset(entry, 0, sizeof(*entry));
173     entry->stop_id = 0;
174     entry->thread_func = thread_pool_worker_thread;
175     entry->thread_arg = pool;
176     SAFE_SNPRINTF(entry->name, sizeof(entry->name), "%s_worker_%zu", pool->name, i);
177 
178     // Create worker thread
179     if (asciichat_thread_create(&entry->thread, entry->name, thread_pool_worker_thread, pool) != 0) {
180       log_error("Failed to create worker thread %zu", i);
181       SAFE_FREE(entry);
182       thread_pool_destroy(pool);
183       SET_ERRNO(ERROR_THREAD, "Failed to create worker thread %zu", i);
184       return NULL;
185     }
186 
187     // Add to thread list
188     mutex_lock(&pool->threads_mutex);
189     entry->next = pool->threads;
190     pool->threads = entry;
191     pool->thread_count++;
192     mutex_unlock(&pool->threads_mutex);
193 
194     log_debug("[ThreadPool] Created worker thread %zu for pool '%s'", i, pool->name);
195   }
196 
197   NAMED_REGISTER_THREAD_POOL(pool, pool->name, NULL);
198 
199   // Register sync primitives for debugging
200   NAMED_REGISTER_MUTEX(&pool->threads_mutex, "threads_mutex", (uintptr_t)(const void *)(pool));
201   NAMED_REGISTER_MUTEX(&pool->work_queue_mutex, "work_queue_mutex", (uintptr_t)(const void *)(pool));
202   NAMED_REGISTER_COND(&pool->work_available, "work_available", (uintptr_t)(const void *)(pool));
203 
204   log_info("[ThreadPool] Created work queue pool '%s' with %zu worker threads", pool->name, num_workers);
205   return pool;
206 }

void
  thread_pool_destroy (thread_pool_t * pool)


Definition at line 208 of file thread_pool.c.


208                                               {
209   if (!pool) {
210     return;
211   }
212 
213   log_debug("Destroying thread pool '%s' (thread_count=%zu, work_queue_mode=%d)", pool->name, pool->thread_count,
214             pool->is_work_queue_mode);
215 
216   // Stop all threads first (if not already stopped)
217   if (pool->thread_count > 0) {
218     log_debug("Thread pool '%s' has %zu threads, stopping them first", pool->name, pool->thread_count);
219     thread_pool_stop_all(pool);
220     log_debug("After thread_pool_stop_all: pool->threads=%p, pool->thread_count=%zu", (void *)pool->threads,
221               pool->thread_count);
222   }
223 
224   // Clean up work queue (in case there's pending work)
225   if (pool->is_work_queue_mode) {
226     mutex_lock(&pool->work_queue_mutex);
227     thread_pool_work_entry_t *entry = pool->work_queue;
228     while (entry) {
229       thread_pool_work_entry_t *next = entry->next;
230       SAFE_FREE(entry);
231       entry = next;
232     }
233     pool->work_queue = NULL;
234     mutex_unlock(&pool->work_queue_mutex);
235 
236     // Destroy condition variable and work queue mutex
237     cond_destroy(&pool->work_available);
238     mutex_destroy(&pool->work_queue_mutex);
239   }
240 
241   // Destroy threads mutex
242   mutex_destroy(&pool->threads_mutex);
243 
244   NAMED_UNREGISTER(pool);
245 
246   // Free pool
247   log_debug("About to free thread pool '%s' at %p", pool->name, (void *)pool);
248   SAFE_FREE(pool);
249 
250   log_debug("Thread pool destroyed (freed memory)");
251 }

size_t
  thread_pool_get_count (const thread_pool_t * pool)


Definition at line 442 of file thread_pool.c.


442                                                         {
443   if (!pool) {
444     return 0;
445   }
446 
447   // Note: We're not locking here for performance
448   // thread_count is a simple size_t which should be atomic on most platforms
449   // If strict thread safety is required, use mutex_lock/unlock
450   return pool->thread_count;
451 }

  pool)

asciichat_error_t
  thread_pool_interrupt_all (thread_pool_t * pool, int sig)


Definition at line 457 of file thread_pool.c.


457                                                                           {
458   if (!pool) {
459     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "pool is NULL");
460   }
461 
462 #ifndef _WIN32
463   // Only available on POSIX systems (Unix/Linux/macOS)
464   mutex_lock(&pool->threads_mutex);
465 
466   if (pool->thread_count == 0) {
467     mutex_unlock(&pool->threads_mutex);
468     log_debug("Thread pool '%s' has no threads to interrupt", pool->name);
469     return ASCIICHAT_OK;
470   }
471 
472   log_debug("Sending signal %d to %zu threads in pool '%s'", sig, pool->thread_count, pool->name);
473 
474   // Iterate through all threads and send signal
475   thread_pool_entry_t *entry = pool->threads;
476   int sent_count = 0;
477   int failed_count = 0;
478 
479   while (entry) {
480     // pthread_kill returns 0 on success, non-zero error code on failure
481     if (pthread_kill(entry->thread, sig) != 0) {
482       log_warn("Failed to send signal %d to thread '%s' in pool '%s'", sig, entry->name, pool->name);
483       failed_count++;
484     } else {
485       sent_count++;
486     }
487     entry = entry->next;
488   }
489 
490   mutex_unlock(&pool->threads_mutex);
491 
492   log_debug("Sent signal %d to %d/%zu threads in pool '%s' (%d failures)", sig, sent_count, pool->thread_count,
493             pool->name, failed_count);
494 
495   if (failed_count > 0 && sent_count == 0) {
496     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_THREAD, "Failed to send signal to any threads in pool '%s'", pool->name);
497   }
498 
499   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
500 #else
501   // Windows: no pthread_kill equivalent needed - socket_shutdown() is sufficient
502   (void)sig; // Unused on Windows
503   log_debug("thread_pool_interrupt_all: no-op on Windows (socket shutdown is sufficient)");
504   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
505 #endif
506 }






References pool.






asciichat_error_t
  thread_pool_queue_work (const char * name, thread_pool_t * pool, void *(*
  work_func )(void *), void * work_arg)


Definition at line 253 of file thread_pool.c.


254                                                          {
255   if (!name) {
256     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "work name is required");
257   }
258 
259   if (!pool) {
260     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "pool is NULL");
261   }
262 
263   if (!pool->is_work_queue_mode) {
264     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "Pool not in work queue mode. Use thread_pool_create_with_workers()");
265   }
266 
267   if (!work_func) {
268     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "work_func is NULL");
269   }
270 
271   // Allocate work entry
272   thread_pool_work_entry_t *entry = SAFE_MALLOC(sizeof(thread_pool_work_entry_t), thread_pool_work_entry_t *);
273   if (!entry) {
274     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate work entry");
275   }
276 
277   entry->work_func = work_func;
278   entry->work_arg = work_arg;
279   entry->next = NULL;
280 
281   // Register work with debug naming system
282   NAMED_REGISTER_THREADPOOL_WORK(entry, name, NULL);
283 
284   // Add to work queue (at the end)
285   mutex_lock(&pool->work_queue_mutex);
286 
287   // Find the last entry in the queue
288   if (!pool->work_queue) {
289     pool->work_queue = entry;
290   } else {
291     thread_pool_work_entry_t *last = pool->work_queue;
292     while (last->next) {
293       last = last->next;
294     }
295     last->next = entry;
296   }
297 
298   // Signal workers that work is available (WHILE HOLDING MUTEX to prevent lost wakeup race)
299   cond_signal(&pool->work_available);
300 
301   mutex_unlock(&pool->work_queue_mutex);
302 
303   log_dev("Queued work to pool '%s' (workers=%zu)", pool->name, pool->num_workers);
304   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
305 }






References pool.






asciichat_error_t
  thread_pool_spawn (thread_pool_t * pool, void *(* thread_func )(void *), void
  * thread_arg, int stop_id, const char * thread_name)


Definition at line 307 of file thread_pool.c.


308                                                              {
309   if (!pool) {
310     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "pool is NULL");
311   }
312 
313   if (pool->is_work_queue_mode) {
314     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "Cannot spawn threads in work queue mode. Use thread_pool_queue_work()");
315   }
316 
317   if (!thread_func) {
318     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "thread_func is NULL");
319   }
320 
321   // Allocate thread entry
322   thread_pool_entry_t *entry = SAFE_MALLOC(sizeof(thread_pool_entry_t), thread_pool_entry_t *);
323   if (!entry) {
324     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate thread pool entry");
325   }
326 
327   memset(entry, 0, sizeof(*entry));
328   entry->stop_id = stop_id;
329   entry->thread_func = thread_func;
330   entry->thread_arg = thread_arg;
331   entry->next = NULL;
332 
333   // Copy thread name (truncate if necessary)
334   if (thread_name) {
335     SAFE_STRNCPY(entry->name, thread_name, sizeof(entry->name));
336   } else {
337     // Auto-generate name
338     SAFE_SNPRINTF(entry->name, sizeof(entry->name), "%s_worker_%d", pool->name, stop_id);
339   }
340 
341   // Create thread
342   if (asciichat_thread_create(&entry->thread, entry->name, thread_func, thread_arg) != 0) {
343     // Save name before freeing entry
344     char thread_name_copy[64];
345     SAFE_STRNCPY(thread_name_copy, entry->name, sizeof(thread_name_copy));
346     SAFE_FREE(entry);
347     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_THREAD, "Failed to create thread '%s' in pool '%s'", thread_name_copy, pool->name);
348   }
349 
350   // Add to thread list (sorted by stop_id)
351   mutex_lock(&pool->threads_mutex);
352 
353   if (!pool->threads || pool->threads->stop_id > stop_id) {
354     // Insert at head
355     entry->next = pool->threads;
356     pool->threads = entry;
357   } else {
358     // Find insertion point (maintain sorted order by stop_id)
359     thread_pool_entry_t *prev = pool->threads;
360     while (prev->next && prev->next->stop_id <= stop_id) {
361       prev = prev->next;
362     }
363     entry->next = prev->next;
364     prev->next = entry;
365   }
366 
367   pool->thread_count++;
368   mutex_unlock(&pool->threads_mutex);
369 
370   log_debug("Spawned thread '%s' (stop_id=%d) in pool '%s' (total_threads=%zu)", entry->name, stop_id, pool->name,
371             pool->thread_count);
372 
373   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
374 }

asciichat_error_t
  thread_pool_stop_all (thread_pool_t * pool)


Definition at line 376 of file thread_pool.c.


376                                                             {
377   if (!pool) {
378     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "pool is NULL");
379   }
380 
381   mutex_lock(&pool->threads_mutex);
382 
383   if (pool->thread_count == 0) {
384     mutex_unlock(&pool->threads_mutex);
385     log_debug("Thread pool '%s' has no threads to stop", pool->name);
386     return ASCIICHAT_OK;
387   }
388 
389   log_debug("Stopping %zu threads in pool '%s' in stop_id order", pool->thread_count, pool->name);
390 
391   // For work queue mode, signal workers to shutdown (BEFORE releasing the mutex to prevent new work)
392   if (pool->is_work_queue_mode) {
393     mutex_lock(&pool->work_queue_mutex);
394     pool->shutdown_requested = true;
395     cond_broadcast(&pool->work_available);
396     mutex_unlock(&pool->work_queue_mutex);
397   }
398 
399   // Save thread list and clear it (WHILE holding mutex to prevent worker state changes)
400   thread_pool_entry_t *threads_to_join = pool->threads;
401   pool->threads = NULL;
402   pool->thread_count = 0;
403 
404   // Release mutex BEFORE joining threads to prevent deadlock if workers need to acquire it
405   mutex_unlock(&pool->threads_mutex);
406 
407   // Now join threads (WITHOUT holding the mutex to prevent deadlock)
408   thread_pool_entry_t *entry = threads_to_join;
409   int joined_count = 0, freed_count = 0;
410   const uint64_t THREAD_JOIN_TIMEOUT_NS = 2 * 1000000000ULL; // 2 second timeout per thread
411 
412   while (entry) {
413     log_debug("Joining thread '%s' (stop_id=%d) in pool '%s' with 2s timeout", entry->name, entry->stop_id, pool->name);
414 
415     // Join thread with timeout to prevent hanging on stuck threads
416     // If thread doesn't exit in 2 seconds, log warning and continue anyway
417     // This prevents reconnection loop from being blocked by deadlocked workers
418     int join_result = asciichat_thread_join_timeout(&entry->thread, NULL, THREAD_JOIN_TIMEOUT_NS);
419     if (join_result != 0) {
420       if (join_result == ETIMEDOUT) {
421         log_warn("Thread '%s' (stop_id=%d) did not exit within 2s timeout - may be stuck. Continuing anyway.",
422                  entry->name, entry->stop_id);
423       } else {
424         log_warn("Failed to join thread '%s' in pool '%s' (error %d)", entry->name, pool->name, join_result);
425       }
426     } else {
427       joined_count++;
428     }
429 
430     thread_pool_entry_t *next = entry->next;
431     log_debug("Freeing thread_pool_entry '%s' at %p", entry->name, (void *)entry);
432     SAFE_FREE(entry);
433     freed_count++;
434     entry = next;
435   }
436   log_debug("thread_pool_stop_all: joined=%d, freed=%d", joined_count, freed_count);
437 
438   log_debug("All threads stopped in pool '%s'", pool->name);
439   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
440 }

    tcp_server_stop_client_threads(), and
  thread_pool_destroy().
